patch.value: Fold bulk and destructive lifecycle variants into their base nouns: update-many into update, close-many into close, delete/restore placement per ADR

update-many and close-many duplicate their base commands as separate top-level entries; delete and restore float top-level. The pm-n7rr target surface maps update (update-many) and close (close-many/delete/restore). NOTE a mapping conflict recorded on ADR pm-pbyu: pm-tqel's history cluster also claims restore (under pm history) — the accepted ADR fixes the single placement and this task implements it. All old spellings remain as hidden aliases via the pm-0z7n shim. Zero feature loss, including update-many dry-run parity (PR#257) and close-many shared-reason/validate-close semantics (pm-i17g). Duplicate check: pm-i17g/pm-wbak/pm-j2ig are closed feature history for the bulk commands themselves; no open consolidation overlap.

update-many and close-many are reachable as facets of update/close with identical semantics including dry-run parity and validate-close; delete and restore live under exactly one noun per the accepted ADR; old spellings alias-dispatch with hints; spelling-parity spec green; contracts, completions, docs, and MCP names regenerated.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Audit #15 scope note: test-all is also a bulk variant of a base noun and belongs in this fold (pm test --all, mirroring update-many->update --many). Include it in the mapping + alias shim so no bulk spelling stays top-level.
